Heterogeneous granular ball-based flexible representations are adaptively developed via multi-scale fuzzy fusion, effectively capturing intricate data manifolds and implicit fuzzy patterns across multi-granularity spaces. Intricate data manifolds are fitted with heterogeneous rather than conventional homogeneous structures to improve the adaptability of representations’ multi-granularity. Implicit fuzzy patterns are extracted with fuzzy approximation operators to mitigate fuzziness and uncertainty in a multi-granularity space. For the first time, granular-ball representation learning and feature selection are adaptively optimized in a unified one-step framework, rather than the conventional two-step frameworks, for flexible adaptability. An effective optimization algorithm with proven convergence is derived. Through the learned high-quality representations, the MFHGBFR manifests superior performance, robustness, and efficiency. Comparative experiments against contemporary state-of-the-art algorithms substantiate these advantages quantitatively and qualitatively.

item.title: ChangeDINO: Bi-level Feature Entanglement Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection with Vision-Language Prior
item.dc:creator: Yupeng Gao
Tianyu Li
Guoqing Wang
Peng Wang
JiayiMa
Yang Yang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: The performance of most existing remote sensing change detection (RSCD) methods is often constrained by limited semantic understanding and a heavy dependence on large volumes of training data. To address these limitations, we propose ChangeDINO, a novel change detection paradigm that effectively integrates both semantic knowledge and positional information from Grounding DINO, a representative open-vocabulary detector. To fully leverage Grounding DINO’s semantic guidance and object localization capabilities, we introduce a bi-level feature entanglement strategy, incorporating two key components: the Semantically Guided Fusion Module (SGFM) and the Position-aware Enhancement Module (PEM). The SGFM adaptively exploits the spatially heterogeneous activation patterns of features from the open-vocabulary detector as semantic guidance signals, selectively amplifying the contribution of high-activation object regions while suppressing uninformative background responses. The PEM, in turn, exploits positional embeddings from cross-modal queries and applies feature-wise linear modulation for pixel-level calibration, thereby enhancing spatial precision and refining boundary localization of changed regions. Together, these mechanisms enable ChangeDINO to transfer fine-grained semantics and precise positional cues from large-scale vision-language pre-training into the RSCD task, achieving state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks—LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and SYSU-CD—as well as on our newly proposed AUUCDD, with F1 scores of 92.89%, 95.79%, 83.87%, and 90.17%, respectively. Furthermore, experiments under data-scarce conditions demonstrate that ChangeDINO maintains superior performance by leveraging Grounding DINO’s rich pretrained representations, thereby reducing dependence on taskspecific annotations and providing a practical solution for data-limited scenarios. The code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/G124556/ChangeDINO. Published: 2026-07-20T20:19:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.773 (consider)

The performance of most existing remote sensing change detection (RSCD) methods is often constrained by limited semantic understanding and a heavy dependence on large volumes of training data. To address these limitations, we propose ChangeDINO, a novel change detection paradigm that effectively integrates both semantic knowledge and positional information from Grounding DINO, a representative open-vocabulary detector. To fully leverage Grounding DINO’s semantic guidance and object localization capabilities, we introduce a bi-level feature entanglement strategy, incorporating two key components: the Semantically Guided Fusion Module (SGFM) and the Position-aware Enhancement Module (PEM). The SGFM adaptively exploits the spatially heterogeneous activation patterns of features from the open-vocabulary detector as semantic guidance signals, selectively amplifying the contribution of high-activation object regions while suppressing uninformative background responses. The PEM, in turn, exploits positional embeddings from cross-modal queries and applies feature-wise linear modulation for pixel-level calibration, thereby enhancing spatial precision and refining boundary localization of changed regions. Together, these mechanisms enable ChangeDINO to transfer fine-grained semantics and precise positional cues from large-scale vision-language pre-training into the RSCD task, achieving state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks—LEVIR-CD, WHU-CD, and SYSU-CD—as well as on our newly proposed AUUCDD, with F1 scores of 92.89%, 95.79%, 83.87%, and 90.17%, respectively. Furthermore, experiments under data-scarce conditions demonstrate that ChangeDINO maintains superior performance by leveraging Grounding DINO’s rich pretrained representations, thereby reducing dependence on taskspecific annotations and providing a practical solution for data-limited scenarios. The code and datasets are publicly available at https://github.com/G124556/ChangeDINO.

item.title: Real-Time Multiple Vehicle Tracking Based on Multivariate Trajectory Prediction With Encoder–Decoder LSTM in Varying Lighting Conditions
item.dc:creator: Igor Lashkov
Shanglian Zhou
Amanda Nitta
Guohui Zhang
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
item.description: In this paper, we consider the problem of multiple vehicles tracking in different complex road scenarios using traffic surveillance videos captured by cameras in varying lighting conditions. We formulate the multiple object tracking problem as a vehicle trajectory prediction, where the goal is to generate a future trajectory sequence of the object in real-time. We propose a lightweight online multi-object tracker based on the Encoder-Decoder LSTM architecture which analyzes patterns underlying the past object trajectory and estimates the vehicle’s motion in subsequent frames. The goal of the developed method is to establish correspondences between vehicle objects within a sequence of video frames. The use of object motion features promotes the generation of high-quality object trajectories and operation in a constantly changing traffic scene environment, with varying traffic flow density and illumination conditions. Tracking results from vehicle trajectories can be used efficiently to estimate the traffic flow parameters, such as traffic volume, vehicle presence, and vehicle movement speed, leading to effective intelligent transportation system (ITS) deployment. To demonstrate the efficiency of the developed approach, the extensive numerical evaluation carried out on multiple traffic scene datasets under diverse conditions demonstrates significant and robust results. During experiments, the tracking performance of the method is measured using specialized MOT metrics against existing tracking algorithms. Our developed approach is competitive against existing resource-intensive trackers and achieves high HOTA and MOTA metrics on publicly available MOT benchmarks. The designed tracking module with an incorporated LSTM network runs at a frequency of 16 Hz, enabling real-time traffic flow monitoring. Published: 2026-07-20T20:21:46+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems Score: 0.773 (consider)

In this paper, we consider the problem of multiple vehicles tracking in different complex road scenarios using traffic surveillance videos captured by cameras in varying lighting conditions. We formulate the multiple object tracking problem as a vehicle trajectory prediction, where the goal is to generate a future trajectory sequence of the object in real-time. We propose a lightweight online multi-object tracker based on the Encoder-Decoder LSTM architecture which analyzes patterns underlying the past object trajectory and estimates the vehicle’s motion in subsequent frames. The goal of the developed method is to establish correspondences between vehicle objects within a sequence of video frames. The use of object motion features promotes the generation of high-quality object trajectories and operation in a constantly changing traffic scene environment, with varying traffic flow density and illumination conditions. Tracking results from vehicle trajectories can be used efficiently to estimate the traffic flow parameters, such as traffic volume, vehicle presence, and vehicle movement speed, leading to effective intelligent transportation system (ITS) deployment. To demonstrate the efficiency of the developed approach, the extensive numerical evaluation carried out on multiple traffic scene datasets under diverse conditions demonstrates significant and robust results. During experiments, the tracking performance of the method is measured using specialized MOT metrics against existing tracking algorithms. Our developed approach is competitive against existing resource-intensive trackers and achieves high HOTA and MOTA metrics on publicly available MOT benchmarks. The designed tracking module with an incorporated LSTM network runs at a frequency of 16 Hz, enabling real-time traffic flow monitoring.

item.title: A Prompt-Guided Transformer Network with Redundancy-Aware Endmember Bundles Considering Spectral Variability for Hyperspectral Unmixing
item.dc:creator: Mingming Xu
Jiaqi Bi
Hongxia Zheng
Shanwei Liu
Hui Sheng
Yanni Dong
Bo Du
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Spectral variability in complex environments brings a dual challenge to accurate hyperspectral unmixing: large intra-class and small inter-class differences. Most deep learning frameworks treat features uniformly and fail to distinguish highly similar endmembers, which easily confuses abundance estimation. To simultaneously tackle this dual challenge, integrating endmember bundle priors that effectively accommodate intra-class variations into deep networks has emerged as a promising solution. However, current methods face bottlenecks in both the extraction and utilization of endmember bundles. To this end, we propose a novel Prompt-Guided Transformer Network (PGT-Net). It accommodates intra-class variations while effectively amplifying subtle inter-class differences. First, addressing the redundancy and noise introduced by existing extraction methods that blindly pursue quantity, a Density-based Redundancy-aware Endmember Bundle Extraction (DR-EBE) algorithm is proposed. It utilizes density-guided clustering and spatial-spectral screening strategy to extract high-quality, low-redundancy endmember bundle priors. Second, our innovatively designed Prompt-Guided Endmember Transformer (PGET) converts these priors into endmember-specific prompt vectors. It performs targeted activation of specific features to capture and amplify subtle inter-class differences. Finally, a Spatial Complementary Integration Module (SCIM) is designed. It utilizes spatial complementarity to adaptively refine feature boundaries and performs cross-channel interactions to further break information isolation. Extensive experiments on multiple hyperspectral datasets show that PGT-Net significantly outperforms state-of-the-art unmixing algorithms. If the paper is accepted, the code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/UPCGIT/PGT-Net. Published: 2026-07-20T20:19:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.772 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Mingming Xu; Jiaqi Bi; Hongxia Zheng; Shanwei Liu; Hui Sheng; Yanni Dong; Bo Du

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3715348

Relevance: 0.772 (consider)

Spectral variability in complex environments brings a dual challenge to accurate hyperspectral unmixing: large intra-class and small inter-class differences. Most deep learning frameworks treat features uniformly and fail to distinguish highly similar endmembers, which easily confuses abundance estimation. To simultaneously tackle this dual challenge, integrating endmember bundle priors that effectively accommodate intra-class variations into deep networks has emerged as a promising solution. However, current methods face bottlenecks in both the extraction and utilization of endmember bundles. To this end, we propose a novel Prompt-Guided Transformer Network (PGT-Net). It accommodates intra-class variations while effectively amplifying subtle inter-class differences. First, addressing the redundancy and noise introduced by existing extraction methods that blindly pursue quantity, a Density-based Redundancy-aware Endmember Bundle Extraction (DR-EBE) algorithm is proposed. It utilizes density-guided clustering and spatial-spectral screening strategy to extract high-quality, low-redundancy endmember bundle priors. Second, our innovatively designed Prompt-Guided Endmember Transformer (PGET) converts these priors into endmember-specific prompt vectors. It performs targeted activation of specific features to capture and amplify subtle inter-class differences. Finally, a Spatial Complementary Integration Module (SCIM) is designed. It utilizes spatial complementarity to adaptively refine feature boundaries and performs cross-channel interactions to further break information isolation. Extensive experiments on multiple hyperspectral datasets show that PGT-Net significantly outperforms state-of-the-art unmixing algorithms. If the paper is accepted, the code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/UPCGIT/PGT-Net.

item.title: Single Image to Textured 3D Object Generation in Frequency Domain: From Theory to Pipeline
item.dc:creator: Qisen Wang
Yifan Zhao
Jia Li
item.description: Single-view 3D reconstruction, also known as image-to-3D, is a persistently challenging task due to the extreme lack of information. Recently, diffusion models pre-trained on large-scale datasets served as 2D priors are used to solve the ill-posed task but suffer from color deviation and view inconsistency, which can be curbed by using diffusion models fine-tuned with 3D annotated data served as 3D priors. However, 3D priors lack high-frequency details, which cannot be solved by direct complementation with 2D priors in spatial domain for introducing erroneous low-frequency 2D prior guidance. In this paper, we revisit the characteristics of different diffusion priors from the frequency perspective. Based on our observations, we theoretically present a unified framework of hybrid optimization using multiple diffusion priors in frequency domain. Under this framework, we further propose Morpheus3D, a pipeline of 3D object generation from any single unposed image in the wild. Morpheus3D enhances 3D prior with high-pass image-prompt 2D prior guidance to reconstruct high-quality 3D objects while effectively suppressing view inconsistency, low-frequency color deviation, and high-frequency lacking problems. Both quantitative and qualitative experiments on the public and our collected datasets with complex textures show that our method exhibits significant improvements in generation quality. Published: 2026-07-19T15:28:52+00:00 Venue: International Journal of Computer Vision Score: 0.771 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Qisen Wang; Yifan Zhao; Jia Li

Journal: International Journal of Computer Vision

DOI: 10.1007/s11263-026-02946-5

Relevance: 0.771 (consider)

Single-view 3D reconstruction, also known as image-to-3D, is a persistently challenging task due to the extreme lack of information. Recently, diffusion models pre-trained on large-scale datasets served as 2D priors are used to solve the ill-posed task but suffer from color deviation and view inconsistency, which can be curbed by using diffusion models fine-tuned with 3D annotated data served as 3D priors. However, 3D priors lack high-frequency details, which cannot be solved by direct complementation with 2D priors in spatial domain for introducing erroneous low-frequency 2D prior guidance. In this paper, we revisit the characteristics of different diffusion priors from the frequency perspective. Based on our observations, we theoretically present a unified framework of hybrid optimization using multiple diffusion priors in frequency domain. Under this framework, we further propose Morpheus3D, a pipeline of 3D object generation from any single unposed image in the wild. Morpheus3D enhances 3D prior with high-pass image-prompt 2D prior guidance to reconstruct high-quality 3D objects while effectively suppressing view inconsistency, low-frequency color deviation, and high-frequency lacking problems. Both quantitative and qualitative experiments on the public and our collected datasets with complex textures show that our method exhibits significant improvements in generation quality.

item.title: An Unsupervised Deep Unfolded Network for Mosaic-based Hyperspectral Fusion Imaging
item.dc:creator: Nan Wang
Aiping Mo
Renwei Dian
Jujue Wei
Shutao Li
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Snapshot hyperspectral imaging based on mosaic sensor enables video-rate acquisition but suffers from inherently low spatial resolution. A practical solution is to fuse the low-resolution (LR) mosaiced hyperspectral image (HSI) with a high-resolution (HR) panchromatic (PAN) image of the same scene. However, recovering a HR HSI from such observations is a severely ill-posed inverse problem, especially in the absence of ground-truth supervision. In this paper, we propose an unsupervised deep unfolded network (UDUN) for the mosaiced and PAN image fusion. To regularize the immense solution space of this ill-posed inverse problem, we exploit the synergy between the DUN and the transformation equivariance prior (TEP), where the explicit degradation modeling capability of DUN is leveraged to deliver an accurate estimation of the spectral response function (SRF), thereby enabling the effective incorporation of TEP as a geometrically consistent and physically grounded prior in unsupervised training. Extensive experiments on three widely-used HSI datasets demonstrate that the proposed UDUN achieves superior reconstruction quality and robustness compared with state-of-the-art fusion methods, highlighting its effectiveness and generalization capability. Published: 2026-07-20T20:19:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.770 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Nan Wang; Aiping Mo; Renwei Dian; Jujue Wei; Shutao Li

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing

DOI: 10.1109/tgrs.2026.3714859

Relevance: 0.770 (consider)

Snapshot hyperspectral imaging based on mosaic sensor enables video-rate acquisition but suffers from inherently low spatial resolution. A practical solution is to fuse the low-resolution (LR) mosaiced hyperspectral image (HSI) with a high-resolution (HR) panchromatic (PAN) image of the same scene. However, recovering a HR HSI from such observations is a severely ill-posed inverse problem, especially in the absence of ground-truth supervision. In this paper, we propose an unsupervised deep unfolded network (UDUN) for the mosaiced and PAN image fusion. To regularize the immense solution space of this ill-posed inverse problem, we exploit the synergy between the DUN and the transformation equivariance prior (TEP), where the explicit degradation modeling capability of DUN is leveraged to deliver an accurate estimation of the spectral response function (SRF), thereby enabling the effective incorporation of TEP as a geometrically consistent and physically grounded prior in unsupervised training. Extensive experiments on three widely-used HSI datasets demonstrate that the proposed UDUN achieves superior reconstruction quality and robustness compared with state-of-the-art fusion methods, highlighting its effectiveness and generalization capability.

item.title: Blind Light Field Image Quality Assessment via Cross-View Encoding and Dynamic Expert Mixture
item.dc:creator: Youzhi Zhang
Jianyu Qian
Deyang Liu
Zeyu Xiao
Xiaofei Zhou
Caifeng Shan
item.description: Blind light field image quality assessment (LFIQA) plays a crucial role in evaluating visual quality without relying on reference images, particularly given the rise of light field imaging in immersive applications. However, the unique spatialangular interdependencies and high-dimensional structure of light field data pose significant challenges to no-reference quality prediction, often leading to suboptimal performance in practical scenarios. To tackle these challenges, we propose a novel blind LFIQA framework based on Cross-Encoding Representations and Dynamic Expert Selection. Our method extracts diagonal sub-aperture images and encode them in a cross-view manner to capture spatial-angular correlations without requiring reference supervision. A Swin Transformer is then employed to model both local and global dependencies, followed by multi-view aggregation using a unified Pyramid Squeeze Attention (PSA) and Spatial- Channel Synergistic Attention (SCSA) module, ensuring the preservation of light field geometry. Finally, a Dynamic Expert Selection mechanism adaptively routes features to specialized expert networks for robust and interpretable quality prediction. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art no-reference image quality assessment methods in both accuracy and generalization, effectively bridging the gap between light field representation and perceptual quality assessment in a reference-free setting. Published: 2026-07-20T20:20:43+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.770 (consider)

Blind light field image quality assessment (LFIQA) plays a crucial role in evaluating visual quality without relying on reference images, particularly given the rise of light field imaging in immersive applications. However, the unique spatialangular interdependencies and high-dimensional structure of light field data pose significant challenges to no-reference quality prediction, often leading to suboptimal performance in practical scenarios. To tackle these challenges, we propose a novel blind LFIQA framework based on Cross-Encoding Representations and Dynamic Expert Selection. Our method extracts diagonal sub-aperture images and encode them in a cross-view manner to capture spatial-angular correlations without requiring reference supervision. A Swin Transformer is then employed to model both local and global dependencies, followed by multi-view aggregation using a unified Pyramid Squeeze Attention (PSA) and Spatial- Channel Synergistic Attention (SCSA) module, ensuring the preservation of light field geometry. Finally, a Dynamic Expert Selection mechanism adaptively routes features to specialized expert networks for robust and interpretable quality prediction. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method outperforms state-of-the-art no-reference image quality assessment methods in both accuracy and generalization, effectively bridging the gap between light field representation and perceptual quality assessment in a reference-free setting.

item.title: Cross-scene Hyperspectral Image Incremental Classification Based on Textual Knowledge Guidance and Dual Distillation
item.dc:creator: Jing Tian
Zhaokui Li
Ke Wang
Yan Wang
Zhuoqun Fang
Xuewei Gong
Kaiwen Lu
Qian Du
item.prism:publicationName: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
item.description: Class-incremental learning (CIL) is of significant value for dynamic Earth observation applications using hyper-spectral images (HSIs). However, existing methods addressing catastrophic forgetting often fail to simultaneously handle limited storage budgets, inter-class semantic ambiguity, and the effective balance between new and old knowledge. To systematically address these challenges, this article proposes a novel class-incremental learning framework based on text guidance and dual distillation (TGDD). First, a distribution-aware sample selection strategy is designed to address the conflict between the inherent intra-class spectral variability of HSI data and limited storage budgets. This strategy adaptively discovers the intrinsic substructures of each class and ensures they are effectively represented in the exemplar set, thereby maximizing the fidelity of historical knowledge under strict storage constraints. Second, a dual distillation mechanism is constructed to achieve a balance between knowledge “retention” and “learning”. Specifically, inter-task knowledge distillation transfers the discriminative ability of the old model to the current one without disrupting consolidated knowledge. Meanwhile, intra-task hierarchical self-distillation promotes collaborative learning between deep and shallow layers, ensuring consistent and highly discriminative feature representations. Furthermore, to tackle misclassification caused by spectrally similar classes across new and old tasks, a multimodal semantic guidance module is introduced. Based on text embeddings of class names, this module dynamically adjusts feature learning and decision boundaries, enhancing the model’s ability to distinguish between spectrally similar but semantically different classes. Extensive experiments on four public HSI datasets demonstrate that TGDD achieves a superior balance between stability (old knowledge) and plasticity (new knowledge). Its classification accuracy and robustness significa... Published: 2026-07-20T20:19:54+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing Score: 0.770 (consider)

This strategy adaptively discovers the intrinsic substructures of each class and ensures they are effectively represented in the exemplar set, thereby maximizing the fidelity of historical knowledge under strict storage constraints. Second, a dual distillation mechanism is constructed to achieve a balance between knowledge “retention” and “learning”. Specifically, inter-task knowledge distillation transfers the discriminative ability of the old model to the current one without disrupting consolidated knowledge. Meanwhile, intra-task hierarchical self-distillation promotes collaborative learning between deep and shallow layers, ensuring consistent and highly discriminative feature representations. Based on text embeddings of class names, this module dynamically adjusts feature learning and decision boundaries, enhancing the model’s ability to distinguish between spectrally similar but semantically different classes. Extensive experiments on four public HSI datasets demonstrate that TGDD achieves a superior balance between stability (old knowledge) and plasticity (new knowledge). item.title: PagedWeight: Efficient MoE LLM Serving with Dynamic Quality-Aware Weight Quantization
item.dc:creator: Yuchen Yang
Yifan Zhao
Anisha Dasgupta
Sasa Misailovic
item.description: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is a popular class of large language models (LLMs), offering high efficiency and accuracy. However, in KV-cache-intensive serving scenarios, MoEs often exhibit a tension between the GPU memory requirements of the model weights and the growing KV cache. We propose PagedWeight, a novel management method for MoE LLM serving that dynamically quantizes MoE model's weights at runtime and balances expert-weight precision with the KV cache sizes. PagedWeight exposes and effectively navigates the complex tradeoff between the model's task accuracy, memory consumption, and throughput/latency. Across several memory-sensitive MoE serving scenarios, PagedWeight improves the quality-memory tradeoff over several existing quantization baselines. PagedWeight achieves FP16-equivalent accuracy with up to 72.0% GPU memory savings and 1.94$\times$ throughput improvement, and improves quality over quantization methods by up to 39.3% at a similar memory budget with at most 4.1% throughput loss. Published: 2026-07-17T17:58:29+00:00 Venue: arXiv Score: 0.770 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Yuchen Yang; Yifan Zhao; Anisha Dasgupta; Sasa Misailovic

Journal: arXiv

Relevance: 0.770 (consider)

Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) is a popular class of large language models (LLMs), offering high efficiency and accuracy. However, in KV-cache-intensive serving scenarios, MoEs often exhibit a tension between the GPU memory requirements of the model weights and the growing KV cache. We propose PagedWeight, a novel management method for MoE LLM serving that dynamically quantizes MoE model's weights at runtime and balances expert-weight precision with the KV cache sizes. PagedWeight exposes and effectively navigates the complex tradeoff between the model's task accuracy, memory consumption, and throughput/latency. Across several memory-sensitive MoE serving scenarios, PagedWeight improves the quality-memory tradeoff over several existing quantization baselines. PagedWeight achieves FP16-equivalent accuracy with up to 72.0% GPU memory savings and 1.94$\times$ throughput improvement, and improves quality over quantization methods by up to 39.3% at a similar memory budget with at most 4.1% throughput loss.

item.title: Human Mesh Reconstruction via Complementary Learning from Event Streams and Frames
item.dc:creator: Liaogehao Chen
Zhenjun Zhang
Yaonan Wang
item.description: Human Mesh Reconstruction (HMR) in complex environments remains challenging, whether using traditional cameras or event cameras. Traditional cameras struggle with motion blur and low-light conditions, while event cameras suffer from sparse foreground activity and a lack of rich textures needed for HMR. In this paper, we introduce EvGHMR, a method that, to the best of our knowledge, is the first to enable mutual compensation between event streams and frames for HMR. Specifically, we introduce Large Vision Models (LVMs) to enhance the discriminability and generalization of frame-based features, enabling robust and reliable HMR. We also propose a complementary learning that explicitly models the correlation between events and frames using cross cost volume and cross-attention based fusion module, enabling effective cross-modal feature interaction. Additionally, we design a dense-flow coherence loss to address the inconsistency between event-based optical flow and the vertex motion of the human mesh. Experiments conducted on the widely-used large-scale datasets MMHPSD and EPMD show that our method achieves about 37.9% higher performance compared to existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) method. Published: 2026-07-20T20:20:43+00:00 Venue: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia Score: 0.769 (consider)
item.content:encoded:
Authors: Liaogehao Chen; Zhenjun Zhang; Yaonan Wang

Journal: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia

DOI: 10.1109/tmm.2026.3714924

Relevance: 0.769 (consider)

Human Mesh Reconstruction (HMR) in complex environments remains challenging, whether using traditional cameras or event cameras. Traditional cameras struggle with motion blur and low-light conditions, while event cameras suffer from sparse foreground activity and a lack of rich textures needed for HMR. In this paper, we introduce EvGHMR, a method that, to the best of our knowledge, is the first to enable mutual compensation between event streams and frames for HMR. Specifically, we introduce Large Vision Models (LVMs) to enhance the discriminability and generalization of frame-based features, enabling robust and reliable HMR. We also propose a complementary learning that explicitly models the correlation between events and frames using cross cost volume and cross-attention based fusion module, enabling effective cross-modal feature interaction. Additionally, we design a dense-flow coherence loss to address the inconsistency between event-based optical flow and the vertex motion of the human mesh. Experiments conducted on the widely-used large-scale datasets MMHPSD and EPMD show that our method achieves about 37.9% higher performance compared to existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) method.
